# Agent Harness Best Practices

> A cited, fact-checked reference on building **agent harnesses** — the runtime/agentic
> loop that wraps an LLM and turns it into an autonomous agent. Language-agnostic
> principles with the Anthropic Claude Agent SDK as the concrete reference, plus
> runnable, type-checked TypeScript and Python code samples. Built from 25 primary
> sources and 150 adversarially-verified claims.

This file follows the llms.txt convention (https://llmstxt.org) to help LLMs and agents
navigate the repository. Read the master guide first; it links to every code sample and
source.

## Core documentation

- [Master guide](docs/agent-harness-best-practices.md): 12 themed sections (agentic loop, tool design, context/memory, system prompts, subagents, permissions/safety, hooks, resilience/streaming, observability, evals, file structure, Claude Agent SDK), plus a decision cheat-sheet and anti-patterns. Every claim is cited.
- [README](README.md): repository overview, structure, and how the research was produced.
- [Code samples index](code_samples/README.md): maps each runnable sample to a guide section.

## Code samples (runnable, type-checked)

- [TypeScript: minimal agent loop](code_samples/typescript/01-minimal-agent-loop.ts): the agentic loop from scratch on the raw Messages API.
- [TypeScript: tool result contract](code_samples/typescript/02-tool-result-contract.ts): poka-yoke args, errors-as-data, bounded output, response_format.
- [TypeScript: Agent SDK quickstart](code_samples/typescript/03-agent-sdk-quickstart.ts): query(), maxTurns/maxBudgetUsd, result subtypes.
- [TypeScript: custom MCP tools](code_samples/typescript/04-custom-tools-mcp.ts): in-process MCP server, tool naming, allow rules.
- [TypeScript: subagents & orchestration](code_samples/typescript/05-subagents-orchestration.ts): context isolation; when not to go multi-agent.
- [TypeScript: permissions & hooks](code_samples/typescript/06-permissions-and-hooks.ts): the permission pipeline, canUseTool, deny rules.
- [TypeScript: context & memory](code_samples/typescript/07-context-and-memory.ts): compaction, context editing, a hardened memory-tool handler.
- [TypeScript: streaming](code_samples/typescript/08-streaming.ts): correct streamed-message reassembly.
- [TypeScript: observability](code_samples/typescript/09-observability.ts): OpenTelemetry config + app-level tracing.
- [TypeScript: eval harness](code_samples/typescript/10-eval-harness.ts): isolated trials, outcome grading, pass^k.
- [Python: minimal agent loop](code_samples/python/01_minimal_agent_loop.py)
- [Python: tool contract](code_samples/python/02_tool_contract.py)
- [Python: Agent SDK quickstart](code_samples/python/03_agent_sdk_quickstart.py)

## Optional / raw material

- [Raw research overview](raw_outputs/README.md): how the evidence is organized (search → sources → claims → synthesis) and the join keys between them. Read this first if you're verifying claims.
- [Source index](raw_outputs/sources/INDEX.md): crosswalk from each guide citation key (`[be]`, `[wt]`, …) to its fetched source file, URL, and claim count. Machine-readable: [sources.json](raw_outputs/sources/sources.json).
- [Findings digest](raw_outputs/synthesis/findings-digest.md): granular 12-theme digest with per-claim source attribution.
- [Verified claims ledger](raw_outputs/claims/verified-claims.json): 150 surviving claims (+ 4 dropped), each with a `sourceId` (→ a source file), `sourceUrl`, and supporting quote. Heaviest artifact (~100KB) — load when you need claim-level provenance.
- [Fetched sources](raw_outputs/sources/): full extracted text of all 25 primary sources (see the index above).
- [Search dumps](raw_outputs/search/): per-angle web-search results (13 research angles).
